item.title: How Iran could try to control the Strait of Hormuz — and profit from it
item.description: Some experts say the Strait of Hormuz will not return to its pre-war state, so what could the future of this vital shipping lane look like?

item.title: Why Florida stayed loyal to Britain during the American Revolution
item.description: Historians explain why East and West Florida remained loyal to Britain instead of joining the 13 colonies in declaring independence.

item.title: With indirect U.S.-Iran talks resuming, Vance says "core mission" achieved
item.description: Vance says the U.S. has accomplished its "core mission" in Iran, as Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner meet mediators in Qatar, but not Iranians.

item.title: Labor activist Dolores Huerta says Trump "does not know history"
item.description: Longtime labor activist Dolores Huerta says President Trump's disparaging remarks about Mexicans show he "does not know history," and called on Latinos in California, Texas and other states to push for change.

item.title: Trump's crypto earnings surge to $1.4 billion in first year back in office
item.description: President Trump's financial disclosure laid out how his crypto-related ventures netted him over a billion dollars in the last year. CBS News' Aaron Navarro breaks down Trump's earnings.

item.title: Democratic socialist Melat Kiros beats Diana DeGette, may become first Gen Z congresswoman
item.description: Democratic socialist Melat Kiros, 29, toppled longstanding incumbent Rep. Diana DeGette in Colorado's First Congressional District, according to a CBS News projection. CBS News' Anthony Salvanto has more details.

item.title: This number helps explain why many Americans are down on the economy
item.description: American workers' share of the nation's income is at its lowest point in almost 80 years, as more of the economy's gains flow to corporations and investors.

item.title: Millions of Americans could experience hottest July 4th ever
item.description: Extremely dangerous heat, coupled with humidity, could result in heat index readings of 100 to 115 degrees from the Midwest to the East Coast, forecasters said.

item.title: Victor Willis, Village People lead singer, dies at 74
item.description: Victor Willis, lead singer of the disco group Village People, whose hit "Y.M.C.A." became a fixture at rallies for President Trump, has died at the age of 74, his wife and the band said.

item.title: American icons reflect on the ideals the U.S. was founded on
item.description: Leaders in the arts, science and business, including Sundar Pichai, Jamie Lee Curtis, Katie Ledecky and Ken Burns shared their reflections on America.

item.title: New disease threats follow Trump administration's health program cuts
item.description: From screwworm to flesh-eating bacteria, mounting public health risks are emerging in the wake of deep cuts to federal health agencies and programs.

item.title: At least 14 kids killed as Pakistan tutoring center's roof collapses
item.description: The roof of a private tutoring center in Lahore, Pakistan, collapsed, killing more than a dozen pupils and leaving at least eight more injured, authorities say.

item.title: Phil Weiser projected to win Democratic primary for Colorado governor
item.description: Colorado Attorney General Phil Weiser is the winner in the pivotal race to be the Democratic nominee for governor in Colorado, AP projects.

item.title: Melat Kiros projected to defeat Diana DeGette in Colorado Congressional primary
item.description: Challenger Melat Kiros, a democratic socialist, is the projected winner of the Democratic primary in Colorado's First Congressional District.

item.title: The 2026 FIFA World Cup schedule and how to watch
item.description: With 104 World Cup games being played in the U.S., Canada and Mexico, it's like "a Super Bowl every single day for five weeks," U.S. team captain Tim Ream told CBS News.

item.title: Trump announces first-ever Republican midterm convention in Dallas
item.description: President Donald Trump has announced that Republicans will hold their first-ever national convention ahead of the midterm elections in Dallas.

item.title: Lawyer for E. Jean Carroll says Trump wants to delay $5 million payment
item.description: Lawyers for President Trump asked for E. Jean Carroll's consent to delay the $5 million awarded to her by a 2023 jury, according to an attorney for the writer.

item.title: DOJ investigating Sen. Ruben Gallego's use of campaign funds, sources say
item.description: The probe focuses on Gallego's use of campaign funds for family trips, a source told CBS News. He has denied wrongdoing.

item.title: Anthropic says Trump admin. lifted restrictions on powerful Claude AI models
item.description: Artificial intelligence giant Anthropic says the federal government lifted a set of restrictions on its powerful Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models, resolving a weekslong dispute.

item.title: Rescuers in Venezuela race to save security guard trapped under building
item.description: Hernan Gil Flores is believed to still be in the security booth of the building's underground parking garage. His wife says rescuers have been able to make contact with him and get him water.

item.title: How athletes like Lionel Messi, LeBron James and Serena Williams are extending their careers
item.description: From the tennis court to the soccer pitch, some elite athletes are proving that their careers don't have to end as they get older. At the World Cup, two of the biggest stars are 39-year-old Lionel Messi and 41-year-old Cristiano Ronaldo. 41-year-old NBA star LeBron James is looking to add another NBA title to his resume, and 44-year-old Serena Williams took to the court at Wimbledon this year. Matthew Futterman, senior writer for tennis at the Athletic, joins "The Daily Report" to discuss.

item.title: Trump made over $1 billion on crypto ventures last year, disclosure shows
item.description: President Trump earned more than a billion dollars from crypto-related ventures alone last year, according to a financial disclosure, including from his meme coin business and his family's cryptocurrency firm.

item.title: Great grandson of Wong Kim Ark says birthright citizenship ruling is "a victory for America"
item.description: The Supreme Court upheld the Constitution's guarantee of birthright citizenship on Tuesday. The court voted 6-3 to strike down President Trump's executive order drastically scaling back that right. Norman Wong, the great-grandson of Wong Kim Ark, who's case nearly 130 years ago led to children born on U.S. soil being granted U.S. citizenship immediately, discusses the ruling.

item.title: Boyle Heights residents outraged after LAPD tows vehicles amid warehouse fire emergency
item.description: After nearly two weeks of emergency vehicles, the smell of rotting food and a massive warehouse fire impacting Boyle Heights residents, some families are now facing extra financial strain after their vehicles were towed by Los Angeles police in the midst of the firefighting efforts.

item.title: Maps show heat dome forecast to scorch major U.S. cities this week
item.description: A heat wave will blast much of the eastern U.S. this week, and forecasters say temperatures will feel even hotter because of the high humidity that's arriving with it.

item.title: Iran's top negotiator says country ready for war as peace talks inch along
item.description: A long-term U.S.-Iran peace deal may depend on a separate agreement between Israel and Lebanon. Analysts say that presents a problem.

item.title: 6/30: CBS Evening News
item.description: President Trump calls Supreme Court's birthright citizenship ruling "too bad"; extreme heat settling over U.S. heading into July Fourth.

item.title: New student loan rules take effect July 1. Here's what to know.
item.description: Loan revamp affects how much students and families can borrow to pay for college, as well as their repayment options.

item.title: 6/30: The Takeout with Major Garrett
item.description: Supreme Court upholds birthright citizenship, transgender athlete bans; Democratic Socialists of America to be tested in Colorado primary.

item.title: Supreme Court takes up challenges to AR-15 bans
item.description: The Supreme Court agreed to take up challenges to so-called assault-weapons bans in Cook County, Illinois, and Connecticut.
